audit_items.proof_trace: UTF8bvnormalize (L239) calls uccompatdecomp without checking its return value; on OOM, uccompatdecomp sets ucsout=NULL and returns -1 (ucdata.c:944-946). The non-approx else-branch then calls uccanoncomp(NULL, -1) (ucdata.c:677), which unconditionally dereferences str[0] before any length guard, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash. UTF8bvnormcmp has the identical pattern at L422 and L441 for both ucsout1 and ucsout2.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker sends LDAP search or compare requests referencing non-ASCII Unicode attribute values (e.g., cn with multibyte chars) under memory-pressure conditions; the server's string normalization path (schema_init.c:1896) calls UTF8bvnormalize, which calls uccompatdecomp; on allocation failure uccanoncomp dereferences a NULL pointer and crashes slapd (DoS).
